article_title: Meta Cuts 700 Jobs Across Reality Labs and Facebook as AI Pivot Drives the Company's Largest Restructuring Since 2023

content_preview.title: Meta Cuts 700 Jobs Across Reality Labs and Facebook as AI Pivot Drives the Company's Largest Restructuring Since 2023
content_preview.summary: Meta lays off roughly 700 employees across five divisions while weighing far deeper cuts of up to 20 percent of its workforce to offset AI infrastructure spending that could reach $135 billion this year.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Meta began notifying approximately 700 employees on March 25 that their positions are being eliminated, according to [NBC News](https://www.nbcnews.com/tech/social-media/meta-layoffs-hundreds-employees-five-divisions-vr-ai-who-rcna265127). The cuts span five divisions -- Reality Labs, Facebook product teams, recruiting, sales, and global operations -- and mark the latest wave of job reductions at the company as it channels resources toward artificial intelligence infrastructure.
